Kemal Özkırşehirli

MIT B.Sc. candidate · Computer Science and Engineering · Chemical Physics · Mathematics

Cambridge, MA, USA - kemalozk@mit.edu

Highlights:

  • MIT B.Sc. candidate, expected May 2027; GPA 5.0/5.0; majors in Computer Science and Engineering, Chemical Physics, and Mathematics; minors in Philosophy and Writing.
  • Current work spans protein foundation model safety, ChromoGen-style 3D chromatin diffusion, autoimmune target discovery, TBXT CADD, ChemAgent-QSM, and EVEdesign protein design.
  • Medalist in the International Chemistry Olympiad and International Mendeleev Chemistry Olympiad; TÜBİTAK National Chemistry Olympiad silver medalist.

I work across AI/ML for science, computational chemistry, biomolecular simulation, protein design, CADD, free-energy methods, statistical mechanics, generative models, and scientific workflow engineering. My current work turns complex molecular, genomic, and physical systems into computable representations, then extracts the rules that make them useful for scientific decision-making.

Professional

About me - Professional - Education - Contact - CV (pdf)

Research - Protein Safety - CADD - Chromatin - Quantum/Stat Mech

Teaching - 6.1200[J] - Berker Group - Mentoring

Writing - Essays - AI for Science notes - RSS

AI/ML for Science - Selected Work

Protein Models and Safety

PRISM - red-teaming protein foundation models for biological plausibility, structure/function constraints, uncertainty, and developability

EVEdesign - uncertainty-aware multi-objective protein and antibody design workflows

Antibody RL - structure-informed cross-attention transformer and PPO pipeline for antibody-antigen interactions

CADD and Drug Discovery

TBXT - 570-compound novelty-filtered CADD funnel for chordoma/TBXT site-F candidates

V2M - autoimmune genetics-to-mechanism target-discovery engine

Pedal AI - LangGraph/FastAPI chemistry agent backend and retrosynthesis workflow platform

Genome, Chromatin, Diffusion

ChromoGen V2 - conditional diffusion for small-molecule-perturbed single-cell 3D chromatin ensembles

Biochemical Diffusion - PINN/neural-ODE stochastic simulation of nonequilibrium reaction-diffusion networks

Theory, Simulation, Agents

ChemAgent-QSM - LLM-guided quantum/statistical-mechanics pipelines using PySCF and auditable Python workflows

Renormalization Group - GNN/PyTorch framework for spin systems, fixed points, and phase boundaries

DFT → kMC - multi-scale computational organic chemistry pipeline linking DFT to kinetic Monte Carlo

Education and Awards

MIT - CS&E, Chemical Physics, Mathematics; Philosophy and Writing; expected May 2027

Olympiads - IChO, Mendeleev, TÜBİTAK, Istanbul Science Olympiads, Waterloo distinctions

Teaching - 6.1200[J] Discrete Math teaching fellow and grader
